away.starter_source: live ESPN roster/stat/injury APIs; starters inferred by live minutes + production

players.name: Brittney Griner
players.injury_comment: A nagging left rib strain is keeping Griner sidelined for a second consecutive contest. Her next chance to play comes Saturday versus the Fever. Olivia Nelson-Ododa stands out as the most likely candidate to start for the Sun on Wednesday.

players.name: Aneesah Morrow
players.injury_comment: Morrow is sidelined for a second consecutive contest due to a left leg strain. She can be considered day-to-day ahead of Saturday's matchup against the Fever. Olivia Nelson-Ododa replaced Morrow in the starting lineup during Monday's loss to the Liberty, logging a season-high 15 points and eight rebounds in 26 minutes.

players.name: Hailey Van Lith
players.injury_comment: Van Lith is nursing a left ankle contusion, which is keeping her out for at least one contest. Her next chance to play arrives Saturday, when the Sun host the Fever.

home.starter_source: live ESPN roster/stat/injury APIs; starters inferred by live minutes + production

players.name: Temi Fagbenle
players.injury_comment: Fagbenle will miss an 11th consecutive contest due to a right shoulder sprain and remains without a definitive timetable for a return to action. She can be considered doubtful for Friday's game against the Mystics until Toronto offers an update on her status.

players.name: Kiki Rice
players.injury_comment: Rice is sidelined for a second consecutive contest due to a Grade 2 left ankle sprain. Her next chance to play comes Friday, when the Tempo visit the Mystics. Julie Allemand drew her fourth start of the season in Sunday's win over the Sky in Rice's stead and should continue to be more involved Wednesday.
